items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Mogaveera Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated April 29, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand only) on Mogaveera Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy – Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by RBI with reference to i

items.title: RBI reiterates caution against unauthorised and misleading campaigns promising Loan Waivers
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) draws attention of the public to Press Release dated December 11, 2023 cautioning members of the public on false and misleading campaigns promising loan waivers. RBI has observed, with serious concern, the continued occurrence of such campaigns, through various media channels and direct outreach, by certain individuals and entities. Such campaigns not only mislead the general public but also interfere with the orderly functioning of the credit system of the country. 2. These campaigns, among others, involve (i) false promises of waiver of outstanding dues to ban

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Pandharpur Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pandharpur, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBl) has, by an order dated April 29, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹2 lakh (Rupees Two Lakh only) on Pandharpur Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pandharpur, Maharashtra (the bank), for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Asset classification and treatment of accounts as Non-Performing Assets’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by the RBI with ref

Yavatmal, Maharashtra, vide Directive No. NGP.DOS.SSM3.No.S611/5-04-166/2025-2026 dated November 06, 2025, for a period of six months up to the close of business on May 07, 2026. 2. items.title: Shri Rohit Jain takes over as RBI Deputy Governor
items.summary: In pursuance of the Government of India notification dated May 02, 2026, Shri Rohit Jain assumed charge as Deputy Governor of Reserve Bank of India today (May 04, 2026) for a period of three years or until further orders, whichever is earlier. Shri Jain was Executive Director of the Reserve Bank before being elevated to the post of Deputy Governor. As Deputy Governor, Shri Jain will look after Department of External Investments and Operations, Financial Markets Regulation Department, Internal Debt Management Department, Foreign Exchange Department, Fintech Department, Risk Monitoring Departmen

items.summary: Consequent on the appointment and assumption of charge by Shri Rohit Jain as Deputy Governor, the distribution of portfolios among the Deputy Governors with effect from May 4, 2026 will be the following: Name Departments Shri Swaminathan Janakiraman* 1. Central Security Cell 2. Consumer Education and Protection Department 3. Department of Supervision 4. Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation 5. Financial Inclusion and Development Department 6. Human Resource Management Department 7. Inspection Department 8. Legal Department 9. Premises Department 10. Right to Information (RIA) Divi

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live Updates: Stocks subdued, rupee at record low as rising oil prices keep investors on edge
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: Indian markets trade lower, tracking weak global cues as oil prices stayed above $100 amid Strait of Hormuz tensions, with Gift Nifty signalling a gap-down and sentiment remaining cautious.

items.title: HSBC first-quarter pre-tax profit misses estimates on higher expected credit losses
items.summary: Europe's largest lender HSBC on Tuesday reported first-quarter pre-tax profit of $9.4 billion, marginally missing analysts' estimates.

items.title: BHEL shares extend rally after strong Q4 earnings beat, brokerages remain divided
items.summary: The stock has been reacting positively to a sharp jump in profitability, healthy order inflows, improved cash flows and margin expansion, reinforcing investor confidence in the company’s turnaround story.

items.summary: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO GMP today: According to market observers, shares of the company are available at a premium of ₹2 in the grey market today

items.title: Dynacons Systems share price jumps 18% after RBI order worth ₹750.82 crore
items.summary: Dynacons Systems shares rose 18% on 5 May after securing a ₹750.82 crore order from the RBI for a private cloud infrastructure project. The contract spans five years, involving design, implementation, and ongoing support for a new data centre in Odisha.

items.title: Amba Auto Sales share price makes flat debut, lists at ₹134.50 on NSE SME
items.summary: Amba Auto Sales share price made flat debut in the Indian stock market on Tuesday, 5 May. The stock listed at ₹134.50 on NSE SME, as compared to the IPO price of ₹135.

items.title: L&T bags 'large' BCGCL order for coal-to-chemicals plant in Odisha, 2,000 TPD capacity
items.summary: Larsen & Toubro has secured a landmark deal to develop a state-of-the-art coal-to-ammonium nitrate facility in Odisha, poised to deliver 2,000 tonnes of ammonium nitrate daily. This venture not only bolsters India's aspirations for enhanced coal gasification but also reduces dependency on imports, perfectly aligning with the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative.

items.title: Biocon’s Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw chooses niece Claire Mazumdar as successor: Report
items.summary: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw has announced her successor. Niece Claire Mazumdar will lead the company. Claire Mazumdar is the founder and CEO of Bicara Therapeutics. She holds advanced degrees and has prior industry experience. Biocon is also undergoing structural changes. The company focuses on biosimilars and integrating artificial intelligence. Other leadership transitions are also in progress across group companies.

items.title: FII selling to keep markets nervous; infrastructure best medium-term bet right now: Sunil Subramaniam
items.summary: Indian stock markets are currently in a complex tug of war. Foreign investors remain cautious due to global inflation concerns. Domestic investors are selectively investing. Infrastructure is identified as a strong medium-term investment theme driven by government spending and private sector growth. While recent political wins offer policy continuity, they are not immediate market movers.

items.title: US Stock Market: Amazon.com Inc. logistics expansion sparks broad selloff in US transport stocks
items.summary: U.S. transportation stocks plummeted Monday as Amazon expanded its logistics services, intensifying competition across parcel delivery, air freight, and trucking. Major players like FedEx and UPS saw significant declines, reflecting investor concerns about Amazon's growing disruption of the traditional transportation ecosystem. The selloff occurred as the sector was nearing record highs, making it vulnerable to such a correction.

items.title: Crude oil futures decline after US-flagged vessels pass through the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: At 9.59 am on Tuesday, July Brent oil futures were at $112.98, down by 1.28%, and June crude oil futures on WTI (West Texas Intermediate) were at $104.12, down by 2.16%

items.title: Australia central bank raises rates to highest since 2024 as inflation stays elevated
items.summary: Inflation had picked up materially in the second half of 2025, and the conflict in the Middle East had resulted in sharply higher fuel and commodity prices.

items.title: Oil slides as traders assess Middle East developments with Iran attacks keeping markets jittery
items.summary: Oil prices declined on Tuesday, retreating from sharp gains in the previous session.

items.title: Global Markets | Resilience Tested: Asian economies balance buffers against rising oil shock
items.summary: Asian economies are scrambling to manage an energy shock caused by the Iran war, with soaring oil prices and supply disruptions hitting the region hard. Governments are deploying subsidies, tapping reserves, and imposing controls, but at rising fiscal costs. Growth forecasts have been cut while inflation expectations have increased, reflecting higher energy prices.

items.title: Manappuram Finance shares fall 3% after Q4 earnings: Here's why Jefferies, other brokerages are bullish
items.summary: Manappuram Finance shares dipped despite a strong Q4 FY26 profit, driven by a surge in gold loans. While brokerages like Jefferies turned bullish, upgrading to 'Buy' with a higher target, others like Morgan Stanley maintained caution. The company announced an interim dividend alongside its results, with the gold loan segment significantly boosting revenue.

items.title: Eternal, Infosys, 8 other shares make Motilal Oswal’s Nifty stock idea list. Do you own any?
items.summary: Motilal Oswal has identified 10 Nifty stocks with strong upside potential, including Eternal and Infosys. Following India's underperformance and FII outflows in FY26, a favorable base is set for equities. A resolution to the Iran-Israel conflict is expected to boost sentiment and help markets recover.

items.title: IPO investors brace for 83 lock-in expiries worth $55 billion in three months. Do you own any?
items.summary: IPO investors are bracing for a significant wave of lock-in expiries between May and August, with shares of 83 newly-listed companies valued at $55 billion becoming eligible for trade. This period will see major companies like Lenskart, Groww, and Pine Labs experience their lock-in periods ending, potentially impacting market dynamics.

items.title: Sobha share price rallies 9.5% after strong Q4 as profit doubles, sales hit record levels
items.summary: Sobha Ltd shares rose 9.5% after reporting a 124% increase in Q4 net profit to ₹91.83 crore, with revenue up 60.2% to ₹1,908 crore. The company also announced a ₹6 dividend and record sales bookings of ₹8,136 crore for FY26.

items.title: India's retail car sales hit record in April, but Mideast crisis clouds outlook
items.summary: Indian car sales reached an all-time high in April. Passenger vehicle sales saw a significant jump. This growth was driven by rate cuts, easier financing, and strong demand from rural areas. However, concerns about Middle East tensions and rising fuel prices may affect demand in the coming months. Dealers are monitoring inventory levels and urging caution from manufacturers.

items.title: US Stock Market: US equity fund inflows hit six-week low amid oil price surge and rate uncertainty
items.summary: Investor demand for US equities weakened, with fund inflows falling to a six-week low of $911 million. Rising oil prices and uncertainty around interest rates, especially ahead of the Federal Reserve policy decision, dampened risk appetite and made investors more cautious.

items.title: Wockhardt shares jump 9% after strong Q4 turnaround, robust FY26 growth
items.summary: Wockhardt shares saw a significant jump on Tuesday. The pharmaceutical company reported a strong turnaround in its March quarter performance. Net profit turned positive, and revenue saw a substantial increase. Full-year earnings also showed improvement. Biotech operations were a key growth driver, with expansion in emerging markets. The company also expanded its product pipeline with new filings and approvals.

items.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO Day 3: GMP slips under 1%; overall subscription at 60%. Key details
items.summary: The Rs 926 crore IPO of OnEMI Technology Solutions has seen moderate demand so far, with overall subscription at 60% by Day 2. Interest is largely driven by institutional investors, while retail participation remains weak. A subdued grey market premium below 1% signals cautious investor sentiment toward the issue.

items.title: Crude oil prices retreat after gaining 6% amid ongoing US-Iran war. What's next?
items.summary: MCX crude oil prices were trading 0.69% lower at ₹9,985 per barrel. Here's what's weighing on crude oil prices, along with its near-term outlook.

items.title: Crude shock, Hormuz fears drag Sensex, Nifty lower at open; ONGC, Adani Ports gain
items.summary: Sensex opened at 77,103.72 and was at 76,995.51, down 273.89 points (0.35%) at 9:16 am; The Nifty 50 opened at 24,052.60 and fell to 24,017.90, down 101.40 points (0.42%)

items.title: Tata Technologies share price jumps 9% after Q4 results. Do you own?
items.summary: The Tata Group stock opened at ₹602.05 per share today, as compared to previous close of ₹591.05 on Monday. Here's Q4 results snapshot, along with its share price trend.

items.title: Gold rises marginally, silver opens flat as traders assess Iran war peace talks. Key levels to watch today
items.summary: Gold and silver prices remained largely flat on MCX as investors tracked U.S.-Iran tensions and inflation risks. Elevated crude oil prices and global uncertainty kept markets volatile. Analysts expect range-bound movement with key support and resistance levels in focus ahead of U.S. macro data and further geopolitical developments.

items.title: Tata Tech shares soar over 7% after Q4 results, but Motilal Oswal sees 15% downside; here’s why
items.summary: Tata Technologies shares surged following strong March quarter results. The company reported a net profit of Rs 204 crore, an 8% increase from the previous year. Revenue also saw a significant jump of 22%. This performance has boosted investor confidence. Experts remain watchful of future execution and demand sustainability.

items.title: Multibagger drone stock ideaForge Technology share price jumps 8% to 52-week high on strong Q4 results
items.summary: ideaForge Technology shares surged over 8% to a 52-week high of ₹794.40 after reporting a net profit of ₹60 crore for March, a significant turnaround from last year's loss. Revenue rose over 600%, driving strong investor confidence and a six-session rally.

items.title: Ambuja Cements shares drop over 2% after Q4 results. What Jefferies and Nomura are saying
items.summary: Ambuja Cements shares fell despite strong Q4 results, where net profit jumped 78% year-on-year to Rs 1,830 crore and revenue rose 10% to Rs 10,892 crore. Profit also surged sharply on a sequential basis, but the stock declined, suggesting muted market reaction despite robust earnings.

items.title: FIIs sell Indian equities on 150 of last 240 trading days. What does it say about their return timing?
items.summary: Foreign investors have sold Indian stocks on most trading days recently. This trend is linked to rising oil prices, a weaker rupee, and higher US bond yields. Global capital is also moving towards artificial intelligence themes. Despite this, domestic investors are absorbing much of the selling, supporting the broader market.

items.title: Consumers face more pain as companies threaten price rises
items.summary: Executives warn that a prolonged energy shock would increase pressure to pass on costs to customers

items.title: Why the Iran war might not spur a faster transition to low-carbon energy
items.summary: Increased priority on security of power supply might see some countries double down on fossil fuels

items.title: End of the line: why the Schroder family sold up
items.summary: The sale of the UK’s largest independent asset manager comes after the death of a patriarch and the rise of giant US funds

items.title: Fortress expands in US legal market with personal injury law firm deal
items.summary: Investment manager uses ownership structure designed to work around profession’s ban on outside equity capital

items.title: NSE launches Electronic Gold Receipts to bring transparency, boost participation in gold market
items.summary: NSE stated that the launch, effective from May 4, 2026, is expected to bridge the gap between physical gold and financial markets by offering a regulated and secure platform for trading in gold

items.title: Sensex falls over 250 points, Nifty below 24,100 as US-Iran launch fresh attacks, rupee tumbles to record low
items.summary: Indian stock markets opened lower on Tuesday, with Sensex and Nifty declining due to escalating US-Iran tensions and a record low rupee. Broader markets, however, showed resilience with small and midcap indices trading in the green. Top sectoral losers included PSU and Private Bank indices.

items.title: US Stock Market: US treasury lifts Q2 borrowing estimate to $189 billion on weaker cash flows
items.summary: The U.S. Treasury has increased its borrowing outlook for the current quarter due to softer-than-expected cash flows, now projecting $189 billion in net borrowing. Looking ahead, the Treasury anticipates raising $671 billion in the July-September quarter. Market participants are closely monitoring upcoming announcements for further clarity on borrowing plans.

items.title: Rupee sinks to record low of 95.39 as fading US-Iran peace hopes amplify economic fears
items.summary: Oil-sensitive Asian peers such as the Indonesian rupiah and Philippine peso also ‌weakened on Tuesday

items.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.39 vs USD as fading hopes of US-Iran peace spotlight economic risks
items.summary: The Indian rupee has fallen to a new record low. This happened after U.S. and Iranian strikes in the Gulf region. These events have worried markets and increased concerns for India's economy. The currency's fall impacts oil imports. Other Asian currencies also weakened. Investors are concerned about rising energy prices and their effect on India's economy.

items.title: BJP’s Bengal win sparks rally in state-linked stocks, but can it last?
items.summary: BJP’s win in West Bengal triggered a sharp rally in state-linked stocks on hopes of policy reset, but experts caution that sentiment alone won’t drive durable economic gains.

items.title: Silver price today steady near ₹2.44 lakh/kg as Middle East tensions, Fed cues keep investors cautious
items.summary: Silver prices remained steady amid escalating US-Iran tensions. In India, MCX silver held at ₹2,43,927 per kg, while international spot silver rose 0.1% to $72.76 per ounce. Geopolitical risks support silver, but a firm interest rate outlook limits price gains.

items.title: Recode Studios IPO Day 1: SME IPO opens today; check GMP, subscription status, and other details
items.summary: Recode Studios launched its IPO on May 5, aiming to raise ₹44.59 crore with a price band of ₹150-158 per share. The IPO includes fresh shares and an offer for sale, with allotment expected on May 8 and listing on BSE SME on May 12.

items.title: Power Grid: steady returns ride power boom, but debt clouds outlook
items.summary: India’s transmission giant benefits from a regulated, cash-flow-stable model and rising electricity demand, but fresh borrowing plans put the spotlight on leverage risks

items.title: Gold rate rises on MCX due to value buying, but strong dollar, inflation concerns limit gains
items.summary: Gold price today: Gold rate climbed on the MCX on Tuesday (5 May) morning, largely due to value buying, but strong dollar, inflation concerns capped gains for the yellow metal.

items.title: Secret Service officer arrested for indecent exposure in Miami after Trump golf event
items.summary: Secret Service Officer John Spillman has been placed on administrative leave after his arrest, which in Miami.

items.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.39 against US dollar amid elevated crude oil prices
items.summary: The Indian rupee fell to a record low of 95.39 per dollar on May 5 due to US-Iranian strikes and rising oil prices, increasing dollar demand. Analysts suggest the Reserve Bank of India may need to implement measures to stabilize the currency amidst ongoing economic pressures.

items.title: Auto sector posts strong April numbers with Maruti leading the charge, but FY27 challenges loom
items.summary: India's auto sector saw robust year-on-year growth in April, defying sequential dips attributed to high March bases and supply issues. Maruti Suzuki stood out with month-on-month gains, driven by GST benefits and strong demand across segments. Tractor and two-wheeler sales also showed strength, though auto ancillaries face margin pressure from rising commodity costs.

items.title: India's auto demand to hold steady for 2-3 quarters, but global risks loom over exports, margins
items.summary: India's auto sector is set for continued strong demand for the next 2-3 quarters. Elevated growth is anticipated through CY26. This positive trend is supported by improving affordability and rural sentiment. Key segments like passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and two-wheelers are showing robust volume increases. Electric vehicle adoption is also gaining significant momentum across the industry.

items.title: Stocks to buy for short term: From Crompton Greaves to Polycab- Experts suggest 6 stock picks for next 1-2 weeks
items.summary: Stocks to buy for short term: Experts recommend six stocks, including Crompton Greaves, GMR Airports, and Polycab India, for the next 1-2 weeks, highlighting their potential target prices and stop losses.

items.title: Vedanta among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks witnessed a sharp rise in futures open interest as of May 04, indicating heightened derivatives activity. The surge suggests traders are building fresh positions or adding to existing ones, reflecting stronger conviction and a directional bias in the near-term price movement of these counters.

items.title: Ather Energy shares jump 5% after Q4 net loss narrows to Rs 100 crore. Here's why Nomura sees sharp upside
items.summary: Ather Energy share price gained over 5% after it reported a sharp narrowing of Q4 FY26 losses to Rs 100 crore, supported by strong revenue growth and improved operating metrics. Higher volumes, retail expansion, and product launches like Rizta drove performance, while EBITDA losses declined significantly, signalling improving unit economics and a stronger push towards profitability.

items.title: Cognizant trims shareholder payouts as AI dealmaking gathers pace
items.summary: Cognizant joins TCS and HCLTech in cutting shareholder payouts as it channels more free cash flow into AI-led acquisitions and capability building.

items.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed 60% so far. Check GMP, review, key details. Apply or skip?
items.summary: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO was 60% subscribed by the second day, with QIBs at 1.45 times and retail investors at 16%. The IPO closes on May 5, with a price band of ₹162-171. Analysts have mixed views on its valuation and risks related to unsecured loans.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty 50 | Stock Market LIVE: Sensex drops over 400 points, Nifty 50 below 24,000; auto, realty stocks drag
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty 50 | Stock Market LIVE: Among sectors, losses were seen in Nifty Auto, Nifty Metals, Nifty Private Bank and Nifty Realty indices, while Nifty IT, Nifty FMCG, Nifty Pharma and Nifty Media witnessed gains.

items.title: Bharat Heavy Electricals and 4 other stocks witness 5-year swing high breakout
items.summary: Five stocks from the Nifty200 universe hit a 5-year swing high at the close on May 05, 2026, signalling strengthening bullish momentum. Such price levels reflect sustained buying interest and improving trend strength, though a swing high differs from a confirmed long-term breakout above key resistance levels.

items.title: IndiGo issues travel advisory after missiles launched towards UAE from Iran, airline says 'there may be changes to flight schedules'
items.summary: IndiGo has issued a travel advisory warning of potential flight disruptions following the UAE's report of detecting four cruise missiles launched from Iran. The airline is advising passengers to check flight statuses due to the evolving security situation in the Middle East. Updates will be provided via registered contact details.

items.title: Dr. Lal Pathlabs among 5 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.summary: Five stocks from the Nifty500 universe were flagged in the RSI Trending Up scan on May 4 after gaining over 4%. The indicator turned positive as RSI crossed above 50 from lower levels, signalling improving momentum, strengthening sentiment, and potential short-term buying opportunities in select stocks.

items.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today — 5 May 2026
items.summary: The Indian stock market ended Monday's session on a positive note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.

items.title: Bond yields are rising. Why that could end the stock market’s rally.
items.summary: The Treasury market is at a critical juncture and deepening losses at this point could pull money away from stocks as prices fall and yields rise.

items.title: SpiceJet’s shrinking fleet puts international operations under scrutiny
items.summary: With just 21 planes, SpiceJet risks falling below the regulatory threshold for international operations, which requires airlines to have at least 20 aircraft or deploy 20% of capacity on domestic routes, whichever is higher.

items.title: Stock recommendations for 5 May from MarketSmith India
items.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 5 May. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.

items.title: Stocks to trade: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 5 May
items.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 5 May. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.

items.title: Premji-backed Shubham Housing Finance begins talks for ₹2,000-crore IPO
items.summary: If it does go ahead with its IPO plans, Shubham will join a growing list of specialised housing finance firms looking to tap the public markets as valuations improve and funding conditions ease.

items.title: India’s fertilizer subsidy reaches ₹2.17 trillion in FY26 due to West Asia war, may rise by a fifth in FY27
items.summary: As the war in West Asia disrupts supply chains, India's fertilizer subsidy may exceed ₹2.17 trillion in FY27. This trend raises concerns about fiscal discipline and food inflation.

items.title: Robots move in as waste firms struggle to find staff
items.summary: Humanoid robots are being added to the automation of waste sorting.

items.title: World Cup prize pool nears $900 million as FIFA boosts payouts. Here’s who gets what
items.summary: FIFA's upcoming World Cup is set to be the most lucrative on-record, with a 'record breaking' prize pool on offer, as FIFA shrugs off other controversies.

items.title: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' kicks off summer movie season in style with $77 million opening
items.summary: Disney and 20th Century Studios' "The Devil Wears Prada 2" opened the summer movie season with $77 million in ticket sales.

items.title: Bret Taylor's Sierra raises nearly $1 billion months after last capital push
items.summary: Sierra's $950 million Series E funding round was led by Tiger and Google's GV, with participation from Benchmark, Sequoia, Greenoaks and others.

items.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: China is rewiring the Silicon Valley model — starting in Hong Kong
items.summary: Hong Kong's IPO surge looks to be just getting started, as China's tech boom sees its ecosystem mature.

items.title: Iran attacks UAE; U.S. says it sank boats in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Stock market indices fell, and oil prices rose after the reported attack on the United Arab Emirates by Iran, which came nearly a month into a ceasefire.

items.title: Novo Nordisk’s head start on GLP-1 pills forces investors to rethink Eli Lilly's dominance
items.summary: Early signs from the launch of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill and Eli Lilly & Co.'s Foundayo are making investors rethink the opportunity for GLP-1 pills.

items.title: Rheinmetall’s first-quarter revenues fail to meet expectations
items.summary: German company’s shares under pressure over profitability concerns and questions about the future of warfare

items.title: GameStop stock sinks after surprise eBay takeover bid, Cohen's combative CNBC interview
items.summary: GameStop made a takeover bid for eBay, offering $125 per share in a cash-and-stock deal that values the e-commerce platform at roughly $55.5 billion.

items.title: Circle jumps nearly 20% on Clarity Act compromise that preserves stablecoin rewards
items.summary: Lawmakers over the weekend reached a compromise on the market structure bill known as the CLARITY Act that keeps stablecoin reward programs intact.

items.title: US yields climb as Iran conflict pushes up crude oil prices
items.summary: USA-BONDS/ (UPDATE 1, GRAPHICS):TREASURIES-US yields climb as Iran conflict pushes up crude oil prices

items.title: Dollar higher as US-Iran conflict keeps risk appetite muted
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX:Dollar higher as US-Iran conflict keeps risk appetite muted

items.title: How Chinese carmaker Geely put roots in the U.S.
items.summary: Chinese company Zhejiang Geely Holding Group has big investments in three automakers already doing business in the U.S.

items.title: AP, Reuters, Minnesota Star Tribune among Pulitzer winners for 2025 work
items.summary: AP, Reuters, Minnesota Star Tribune among Pulitzer winners for 2025 work

items.title: Sona Comstar sets sights on acquisitions, EV-focused order book to drive growth
items.summary: Sona Comstar is actively seeking acquisitions to utilize its substantial cash reserves. The auto-components maker is building on a large order book, with a growing emphasis on electric vehicles. The company is open to deals up to ₹2,000 crore or more, targeting technology and new product adjacencies. While domestic acquisitions are preferred, international options are not ruled out.

items.title: Carlyle buys RCM providers Knack & Equalize for $600 million
items.summary: Carlyle Group is building a global healthcare revenue cycle management platform. The firm acquired majority stakes in US companies Knack and Equalize. These companies will merge to form a new entity. Carlyle plans to invest significantly and aims for a public listing this fiscal year. This move strengthens their presence in technology and tech-enabled services.

items.title: Pharma exports surpass $31 billion in FY26 despite global headwinds
items.summary: Indian pharmaceutical exports reached over $31 billion in FY26. March saw a significant 23% drop, primarily due to a slowdown in the United States and China. However, other markets like Africa and Oceania showed strong growth. Vaccines emerged as the fastest-growing export category. The industry is navigating challenges in key markets while exploring new growth avenues.

items.title: NCLT upholds Aanand Rai's plea in row with Eros International
items.summary: The NCLT has permitted filmmaker Aanand L Rai to pursue arbitration in a dispute with Eros International Media. The tribunal deemed Eros's petition a 'dressed-up petition' intended to bypass their arbitration agreement. This ruling favors Rai, directing the matter to arbitration as originally agreed. The NCLT's decision highlights the importance of contractual arbitration clauses.

items.title: BJP gains in Bengal may accelerate reforms push amid economic headwinds
items.summary: The dominant National Democratic Alliance constituent at the Centre has registered its maiden victory in West Bengal, retained Assam and Puducherry (with alliance partners) and won a record three seats in Kerala. It won only one seat in Tamil Nadu, against four in 2021.

items.title: NCLT approves Rs 730 crore revival proposal for Rajesh Hotels
items.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved a revival plan for Rajesh Business & Leisure Hotels. A consortium of Rare Asset Reconstruction Company and Check-Inn Hotels will acquire the company. The plan involves a payment of ₹730 crore. This follows the company's admission into insolvency proceedings in April 2022. Secured creditors will receive a full settlement.

items.title: China's TCL in talks with local companies to sell 51% in Indian plant
items.summary: Chinese electronics giant TCL is looking to sell a majority stake in its Indian display plant. The company is seeking between ₹5,708 and ₹7,611 crore for the divestment. Several Indian firms are in early-stage discussions to acquire the stake. TCL aims to retain a 49% share in the facility.

items.title: RBI flags false loan waiver claims by some, warns of legal action
items.summary: RBI cautioned such campaigns can mislead borrowers into believing their obligations can be written off outside formal processes, which could affect repayment behaviour and weaken credit discipline. “Such campaigns not only mislead the general public but also interfere with the orderly functioning of the credit system of the country,” it said.

items.title: IndiGo ropes in former Airbus executive for AI push
items.summary: IndiGo has appointed Jochen Hoesch, previously at Airbus, to head its AI, data, and analytics. This move highlights the airline's commitment to enhancing operational efficiency and customer experience through advanced technology. IndiGo is investing in its digital infrastructure to stay future-ready.

items.title: Anthropic teams with Goldman, Blackstone and others on $1.5 billion AI venture targeting PE-owned firms
items.summary: The move marks Anthropic's latest effort to deepen its lead in the market for enterprise AI as competition intensifies with rivals including OpenAI

items.title: Direct tax mop-up misses FY26 target; Collections rise 5%
items.summary: India's direct tax collections saw a modest rise in FY26. However, the total mop-up missed the revised target by ₹81,000 crore. Both corporate and personal income tax collections were lower than anticipated. Officials noted the economy's resilience. They expect some moderation in tax growth this year. A clearer picture will emerge by June.

items.title: Middle East crisis day 66 as it happened: US and Iran exchange fire as Strait of Hormuz crisis reignites
items.summary: Oil jumps to $115 and Treasuries sell off as Tehran targets UAE and US hits Iranian fast boats

items.title: PMI rises to 54.7; Inflation pressures mount
items.summary: India's manufacturing sector saw modest growth in April. New orders and production improved, boosting activity. However, rising costs due to the West Asia conflict are a concern. Input prices increased at their fastest pace since August 2022. Output prices also saw a quick rise. Export performance was strong. Hiring activity strengthened, indicating optimism for future expansion.

items.title: Blackstone’s Jon Gray Sees AI Creating ‘Huge Boom’ in Blue Collar Jobs
items.summary: Blackstone Inc. President Jon Gray said the boom in artificial intelligence will lead to a surge in blue-collar employment as asset managers like his shell out billions on data centers and other infrastructure.

items.title: India to testify at USTR overcapacity probe on May 8
items.summary: India will challenge US accusations of excess manufacturing capacity on May 8. Indian trade officials and industry representatives will present their case. The US probe targets sectors like petrochemicals, steel, and solar modules. India denies these claims, stating trade surpluses are macroeconomic phenomena. This hearing is part of a broader US investigation into trade practices.

items.title: NCLAT ends insolvency proceedings against realty firm Embassy Development
items.summary: The Delhi bench of the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), in December 2025, directed the initiation of insolvency proceedings against the realty firm over a plea filed by Canara Bank. The bank had alleged that Embassy Development owed Rs 200 crore as a corporate guarantor to a loan given to Indiabulls Realtech (now Simar Thermal Power).

items.title: RBI’s Rohit Jain takes charge, assumes key portfolios
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India has a new deputy governor, Rohit Jain. He took charge on Monday for a three-year term. Jain now oversees many key departments. These were previously managed by T Rabi Sankar. Shirish Chandra Murmu will handle currency management and payment systems. Jain's extensive portfolio includes IT, fintech, and financial markets.

items.title: Oil rises on signs of heightened tensions in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent spiked more than 5% to trade above $114 a barrel, before paring some of the gain as prices fluctuated sharply

items.title: How Spirit Airlines' demise will benefit rivals — and raise airfares even more
items.summary: Spirit Airlines' collapse will give some carriers more pricing power as they race to capture Spirit's customers.

items.title: RBI clears 99 pc applications within deadline under Citizen's Charter
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India reports significant progress in processing applications. In April 2026, the central bank successfully handled 99.7 percent of applications within the set deadlines. This achievement reflects efficient operations across various functions. A large number of applications were processed, with most meeting the stipulated timelines. The RBI continues to focus on timely service delivery to its stakeholders.

items.title: The oil price crunch is looming
items.summary: The longer the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues, the more likely a crisis-like adjustment in rich economies becomes

items.title: Iran warns US Navy over Strait of Hormuz as UAE intercepts missiles
items.summary: Oil jumps to $114 as tensions flare up in Middle East

items.title: Ambuja Cements resets expansion strategy as Karan Adani flags cost pressures and execution gaps
items.summary: Ambuja Cements said it would defer parts of growth plan, while prioritizing cost discipline and asset stabilization following FY26 revenue miss and margin pressure.

items.title: Trump family crypto venture sues major backer for defamation
items.summary: World Liberty Financial accusation against Justin Sun comes after he sues firm over alleged fraud

items.title: India’s rice purchase reaches near 50 million tonnes, up 6% from a year ago
items.summary: Government may be forced to dipose of the grain via sales to ethanol distilleries and open market

items.title: SEBI proposes alignment of securitisation norms with RBI framework
items.summary: Markets regulator suggests easing restrictions, enhancing disclosures and revising trustee norms

items.title: Q4 Results Today Highlights: Ambuja Cements, Aditya Birla Capital, BHEL, Exide Industries, Petronet, Godrej Properties, Tata Tech, Sobha Q4 profits rise, Ather Energy narrows loss
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, May 4, 2026, Updates: Catch updates

items.title: RBI reshuffles top deck: New DG Rohit Jain takes charge, portfolios realigned
items.summary: Rohit Jain has been appointed deputy governor of the Reserve Bank of India, leading to a portfolio reshuffle among governors. The changes, effective 4 May, aim to enhance oversight of fintech and adapt to evolving financial priorities amid global volatility.

items.title: GameStop makes $55.5bn takeover offer for eBay
items.summary: GameStop's boss Ryan Cohen says he sees potential to make eBay a much bigger rival to Amazon.

items.title: Reliance cuts exports of alkylates, boosts LPG output
items.summary: Reliance Industries is increasing liquefied petroleum gas production. The company is reducing output of alkylates, a gasoline-blending component. This move aims to compensate for lost LPG imports from Middle Eastern countries. India faces a severe cooking fuel crisis. The government has directed refiners to maximize LPG production. This is to ensure supply for households.

items.title: Godrej Properties posts record ₹1,850 crore profit in FY26
items.summary: Q4 FY26 saw ₹7,947 crore in collections and a 70% jump in net profit to ₹650 crore

items.title: Ather Energy cuts losses, moves closer to breakeven as FY26 revenue jumps on scale-up
items.summary: The electric two-wheeler maker cut its net loss to ₹517 crore and pushed up revenue 66% in FY26, driven by strong volume growth and a rapidly expanding store network, even as commodity costs remain a drag.

items.title: India’s net direct tax collections rise 5.12% to Rs 23.40 lakh crore till March 31
items.summary: Net direct tax collections reached approximately Rs 23.40 lakh crore, marking a 5.12% increase as of March 31. This growth was bolstered by a 1% decrease in refunds, which helped offset the 4% rise in gross direct tax collections to Rs 28.1 lakh crore for the financial year. The dip in refunds contributed to a stronger net revenue position.

items.title: India looks to boost LNG tank capacity as Qatar supplies stall
items.summary: India imports about half of its natural gas requirement, which is used to generate electricity, make fertiliser, convert into CNG to run automobiles, piped to household kitchens for cooking and used as feedstock in a variety of industries. This gas is imported in its liquid form, called LNG, in ships from countries like Qatar and the US.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Bajaj Finance (Add)
items.summary: InCred Equities

items.title: No government financial support planned for fuel retailers' petrol, diesel, ATF losses
items.summary: The Indian government has declared its stance not to offer financial assistance to its state-run fuel companies, which are currently in the red due to selling fuels like petrol and diesel at a discount. Despite the pressure of escalating crude oil prices, retail costs for these fuels have stayed steady.

items.title: Warsh's take on Fed independence is met with confusion and some concern
items.summary: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh is putting his own spin on the independence of monetary policy. Many former Fed officials aren't sold.

items.title: South Korean stocks hit fresh record, building on historic monthly rally in April
items.summary: Asia markets were mixed Monday as investors assess news of President Donald Trump's plan to "free" ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Broker’s Call: NLC India (Buy)
items.summary: SBI Securities

items.title: Godrej Properties targets ₹39,000 crore in sales for FY27 after record-breaking year
items.summary: In FY26, GPL's sales bookings rose 16% year-on-year to ₹34,171 crore. That was achieved through the sale of 17,513 units spanning 27 million sq ft, the highest ever by an Indian real estate firm.

items.title: U.S. military denies Iran's claim it struck American warship in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran has blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz since the U.S. and Israel started the war in late February, resulting in supply and price shocks.

items.title: Blackstone and Goldman among backers for $1.5bn JV with Anthropic
items.summary: New consulting company to advise Wall Street groups on how to deploy its AI across their investment portfolios

items.title: RBI cautions against misleading campaigns promising loan waivers
items.summary: Attention all borrowers! The Reserve Bank of India has flagged a concerning trend involving fraudulent loan waiver initiatives. These duplicitous campaigns claim to erase debts owed to banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) but are merely traps set by dishonest individuals aiming to profit through false promises and upfront fees.

items.title: NSE launches electronic gold receipts trading
items.summary: The EGRs can be convereted into physical gold by buyers

items.title: Trial of Blackpool’s block trade king begins in Hong Kong
items.summary: Simon Sadler of Segantii Capital faces criminal charges over alleged insider trading of clothing retailer Esprit

items.title: Solex Energy to invest Rs 4,000 cr to set up solar cell manufacturing plant, BESS project in Gujarat
items.summary: Solex Energy will invest Rs 4,000 crore in Gujarat. The company will establish a solar cell manufacturing facility and a battery energy storage system project. This initiative aims to boost Gujarat's clean-tech manufacturing. It will also create jobs and support India's renewable energy goals. The project includes a 5-GW solar cell plant and a 10-GW BESS project.

items.title: US denies Iranian report warship was struck by missiles
items.summary: It comes as the US said on Monday it will begin to help "guide" vessels out of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Battery raw material lithium may increase further this year
items.summary: However, it will hinge on how the Iran war develops and demand-supply situation

items.title: ADB should step up capital expansion measures, adopt 'more transformational approach': MoS Finance
items.summary: In his address at the ADB Annual Meetings, Pankaj Chaudhary also said that the multilateral funding institution should adopt a "more transformational approach" to support sustainable and resilient regional development amid the current geopolitical challenges.

items.title: ‘Misplaced euphoria’: Markets are sleepwalking into a recession amid Iran war oil price shock
items.summary: As US equities hit fresh highs, strategists warn that investors are downplaying the impact of soaring oil prices.

items.title: EU to water down anti-deforestation rules by exempting leather imports
items.summary: Move to soften impact on companies of flagship climate legislation is a setback for environmental groups

items.title: Carlyle buys majority stake in Knack, EqualizeRCM to build AI-led healthcare billing platform
items.summary: The private equity firm is betting on consolidation in a fragmented US billing services market, with plans to scale via acquisitions and global delivery

items.title: Rupee hits record low as Nifty closes well off day’s high
items.summary: Nifty 50 closed at 24,119, up 121 points or 0.51%; Sensex settled at 77,269, adding 356 points or 0.46%

items.title: Citi appoints Raj Rathi as head of mergers & acquisitions in India
items.summary: Rathi joins Citi from Dream Sports, where he served as head of strategy and corporate development.

items.title: Central Bank of India expects limited impact from new provisioning rules
items.summary: Central Bank of India managing director and CEO Kalyan Kumar says it is well buffered against the RBI’s shift to stricter, forward-looking loan-loss provisioning, with existing provisions and strong capital expected to absorb any transition impact,

items.title: Supervision must look beyond formal compliance, says RBI Deputy Guv Swaminathan J
items.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J. warns that weak oversight in banking can lead to hidden risks. He stresses that supervision must go beyond reported numbers to understand actual business operations. This approach is crucial for protecting depositors and the wider economy. Digital banking brings new challenges requiring judgment and a sense of public purpose.

items.title: Fertiliser subsidy bill set to overshoot budget estimate of Rs 1.71 lakh cr amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: India's fertiliser subsidy bill for fiscal 2026-27 is set to surpass its budget. Rising costs of imported urea and other fertilisers are driving this increase. Despite global supply chain issues, fertiliser availability for the kharif 2026 season remains strong. Domestic production and imports are being managed to meet demand. States are actively stocking fertilisers in advance.

items.title: MRAI asks for dedicated ministry for recycling
items.summary: At the Paryavaran Niti Manthan organised by the Material Recycling Association of India (MRAI) in association with the Niti Aayog on Monday, MRAI has called for called for a central nodal ministry to ensure continuity in leadership, faster decision-making, better inter-ministerial coordination and streamlined compliance processes.

items.title: NCLAT sets aside NCLT insolvency order against Embassy Developments
items.summary: Embassy Developments has won a significant legal battle. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has cancelled an earlier order that placed the company into insolvency proceedings. This ruling brings an end to the corporate insolvency resolution process. The developer's operations and projects remain unaffected. This positive development follows strong financial performance, with substantial pre-sales reported for the fiscal year.

items.title: India must map, monitor economic vulnerabilities in energy, food, other areas: EAC-PM chairman
items.summary: Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) Chairman S Mahendra Dev in an interview with PTI further said the ongoing West Asia conflict highlights the need for a forward-looking approach to risk management.

items.title: India has no plans for financial support for fuel retailers, official says
items.summary: India will not compensate state-run fuel retailers for losses incurred by selling transport fuels below market prices. While companies have increased prices for industrial customers and jet fuel for foreign carriers, retail prices for gasoline, gasoil, LPG, and jet fuel for Indian carriers remain unchanged. The government prioritizes protecting retail consumers.

items.title: Zen Technologies shares tumble as Q4 profit and revenue plunge
items.summary: Company posted a standalone net profit of ₹14.75 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, marking a decline of over 83 per cent y-o-y from ₹84.92 crore

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares fall despite Q4 profit beat, top Nifty 50 loser
items.summary: Brokerages largely acknowledged the earnings beat but offered a mixed outlook

items.title: Sun TV Network falls 9% as DMK trails in Tamil Nadu election trends
items.summary: Market sentiment was also influenced by historical ties between the company’s promoters and the DMK, raising expectations of volatility as counting continues

items.title: Rupee falls 39 paise to close at all-time low of 95.23 against US dollar
items.summary: Crude oil prices at $110 per barrel, foreign capital outflows pressure rupee

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Benchmark indices close firmer; Sensex settles 355 points higher at 77,269, Nifty up 121 points at 24,119
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Updates: Sensex settles 355.90 points or 0.46 per cent positive at 77,269.40, and Nifty 50 rises by 121.75 points or 0.51 per cent to 24,119.30

items.title: 'Bubble effect': Weight loss drug fueled growth is putting the pharma sector at risk, report finds
items.summary: Obesity assets represent about 25% of total forecast sales of the late-stage pipeline, while oncology's share has slipped to 20%, Deloitte found.

items.title: Is the US truly resilient to the energy market strife?
items.summary: ‘Somewhat counterintuitive’ indeed

items.title: IndiGo's Head of Global Sales Vinay Malhotra resigns
items.summary: Vinay Malhotra, IndiGo's Head of Global Sales, has resigned. He will leave his position on July 3. Malhotra is seeking new opportunities outside the airline. This follows recent leadership changes at the country's largest airline. IndiGo previously announced William Walsh as its new CEO and Aloke Singh as Chief Strategy Officer.

items.title: Milky Mist raises ₹482 crore in pre-IPO round led by Temasek arm
items.summary: The entire primary investment was led by Jongsong Investments, while promoters Sathishkumar T. and Anitha S. sold 89,43,903 equity shares at ₹139.76 a share, aggregating roughly ₹125 crore, as part of a secondary transaction.

items.title: MahaRERA cracks down on errant builders; issues notices over 8,212 projects
items.summary: MahaRERA is taking strict action against over 8,000 housing projects. These developers failed to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports on time. This includes many projects in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. MahaRERA has issued show-cause notices. Developers have 60 days to comply. Failure could lead to severe penalties, including project registration cancellation.

items.title: GE Vernova gets order to supply 9 pumped storage units in India
items.summary: GE Vernova will supply nine pumped-storage units for Megha Engineering & Infrastructures Limited's Upper Sileru hydropower plant in Andhra Pradesh. This 1.35-gigawatt project, expected by 2030, will be one of India's largest. Pumped storage is vital for grid stability and balancing renewable energy. GE Vernova's work includes design, manufacturing, and commissioning of the units and control systems.

items.title: Canada’s beef producers say Mercosur trade deal will devastate industry
items.summary: Farmers fear a flood of cheap meat as Carney government looks for entry to South American bloc

items.title: The US decoupling from the global gloom has limits
items.summary: Total economic and financial insulation is an impossibility

items.title: Carlyle acquires Knack RCM and Equalize RCM to create an AI-native, global multi-specialty healthcare RCM platform
items.summary: Global investment firm Carlyle has acquired a majority stake in US healthcare revenue cycle management providers Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM. This move aims to create a global, AI-native, multi-specialty RCM platform. The combined entity will leverage AI and global delivery to enhance operational scale and client outcomes, addressing critical financial risks for healthcare providers.

items.title: India's LPG consumption slides 16 pc in April amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: Cooking gas LPG use in India saw a significant 16 percent drop in April. Supply issues stemming from the West Asia conflict impacted availability for homes and businesses. This led to reduced consumption compared to last year and the previous month. Other fuel sales like diesel and petrol also showed moderated growth.

items.title: Treasury yields edge higher as traders monitor factory data and Middle East developments
items.summary: Treasury yields gained Monday as traders looked out for more U.S. factory data, while monitoring developments in the Middle East.

items.title: Gold futures slide to ₹1.50 lakh/10g as oil surge, dollar weigh on bullion
items.summary: During the previous week, gold had depreciated by ₹1,347, or nearly 1 per cent, to close at ₹1.51 lakh per 10 grams

items.title: WeWork India opens new coworking centre in Delhi with over 1,400 desks
items.summary: WeWork India has launched a new co-working space in Aerocity, New Delhi. This expansion adds 1.1 lakh sq ft and over 1,400 desks to its operations. The company is growing to meet the increasing demand for flexible workspaces. This marks WeWork India's 17th centre in the Delhi-NCR region. The company has a significant presence across eight cities in India.

items.title: Centre plans 3.25 mn ha shift to chemical-free farming by FY31
items.summary: The department of expenditure in coordination with the agriculture ministry, is drafting a proposal to create 65,000 clusters of 50 hectares each under the National Mission on Natural Farming

items.title: Repertoire drinking spurs new spirit launches
items.summary: Nearly a dozen products are either already in market or being introduced through early 2026-27 across whisky, brandy, vodka, gin, rum and tequila.

items.title: India palm oil imports fall to one-year low in April on weak demand
items.summary: India's palm oil imports plummeted 27% in April to a one-year low, driven by sluggish institutional demand and a reduced price advantage over rival oils. This decline could impact global palm oil futures, while imports of soyoil and sunflower oil saw significant increases, pushing overall edible oil imports to a 2026 high.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp appoints Sachin Agrawal as CTO
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp has appointed Sachin Agrawal as its new Chief Technology Officer, effective from May 21. Agrawal will lead the company's research and development transformation. He brings over 29 years of experience in automotive R&D and technology leadership. Agrawal previously worked at VE Commercial Vehicles and began his career at Daewoo Motors.

items.title: India explores steps to mobilise dollar inflows as rupee slides, sources say
items.summary: India's central bank is actively seeking methods to attract dollar inflows. This move aims to bolster foreign exchange reserves and stabilize the rupee. Measures under consideration include reviving a deposit scheme for non-resident Indians and removing withholding tax on overseas government bond investors. These steps are being explored amid rising oil prices and capital outflows.

items.title: How Navi is sharpening its IPO pitch ahead of a fresh FY27 filing
items.summary: Navi Ltd plans to file for an IPO in FY27, focusing on profitability and a diversified business model. The fintech aims to attract investors with its offerings in payments, lending, insurance, and investments.

items.title: Gold edges lower on rate fears, Iran tensions cap downside
items.summary: MCX Gold is currently trading near ₹1,51,000 per 10 grams, pulling back from a recent multi-week high of ₹1,55,000

items.title: Gains narrow by noon; Kotak Bank, Bharti Airtel drag as broader market stays firm
items.summary: Among Nifty 50 gainers, Hindustan Unilever led with a 3.87% rise to ₹2,338, touching a high of ₹2,365.80, on volumes of 15,12,837 shares worth ₹35,368.01 lakhs

items.title: Hindustan Laboratories, RK Steel get SEBI nod for IPOs
items.summary: Both companies had filed their draft papers in September and received SEBI’s observations on April 27, which effectively clears them to proceed.

items.title: Bajaj Auto total sales up 40% at 5,13,792 units in April
items.summary: Bajaj Auto has announced a significant sales surge for April. Total sales climbed by 40 percent, reaching over 5.13 lakh units. This impressive growth was driven by a substantial 83 percent increase in exports. Domestic sales also saw a healthy rise of 13 percent. The company's two-wheeler segment performed strongly, with both domestic and export figures showing robust gains.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: As Gulf fatigue sets in, earnings take center stage
items.summary: As geopolitical developments indicate a stalemate, company earnings have taken center stage.

items.title: Govt has eased FDI norms for foreign cos having small Chinese/Hong Kong stake: An explainer
items.summary: India has eased Foreign Direct Investment rules for companies with up to ten percent Chinese or Hong Kong shareholding. These firms can now invest in India through the automatic route starting May 1, 2026. This change aims to boost investment flows. Previously, stricter rules applied due to security concerns. The government has also expedited clearances for certain manufacturing sectors.

items.title: Our pension funds are not quasi-government money
items.summary: New legislation pushing the mandatory allocation of investments into private assets conflicts with the fiduciary duty of trustees

items.title: India is burning more coal as extreme heat and the Iran war squeeze energy supplies
items.summary: India, the world's third-largest emitter of carbon dioxide, is burning more coal as demand for power rises, most of which is coal-fired.

items.title: 'I had to pay £14k after my cat was run over'
items.summary: People with and without pet insurance are facing large bills when their animals get sick or injured.

items.title: India's factory growth stays sluggish in April amid war-led soaring costs, PMI shows
items.summary: India's manufacturing growth saw a slight uptick in April, reaching 54.7, but remained near a four-year low due to weak domestic demand and rising input costs. Despite challenges, export orders surged, and companies expanded hiring and expressed optimism for future growth.

items.title: Indian private sector new order growth quickens in April but remains slowest in 4 years: PMI survey shows
items.summary: After losing some momentum in March due to West Asia conflict, growth across India's private sector expanded at a quicker rate at the start of new fiscal year in April.

items.title: NCLAT rejects Vedanta's challenge to approval of Adani's resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates
items.summary: Vedanta's challenge to Adani Enterprises' resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd was dismissed by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal. The tribunal found no legal flaws in the creditors' choice, marking a significant setback for Vedanta despite its claims of offering a superior bid.

items.title: AI + Halo = $$$
items.summary: A techno-industrial boomlet

items.title: OPEC+ announces 188,000 barrels-per-day output increase in first meeting without UAE
items.summary: Concerns around production were amplified further last week with news of the UAE's shock departure.

items.title: Indore to host meeting of BRICS agriculture ministers in June
items.summary: Indore will host BRICS agriculture ministers from June 12 to 13. Officials will meet earlier from June 9 to 11. Discussions will focus on food security, smart agriculture, global trade, and farmer welfare.

items.title: What an empty car park tells us about the UK's debt problem
items.summary: The BBC has been speaking to people living in one of England's poorest communities.

items.title: More than double the gas stuck in Hormuz is wasted each year, IEA says
items.summary: Capping methane emissions would alleviate energy and climate crisis

items.title: The nuclear option: Atomic energy could offer Europe hope, say analysts — but it won't be easy
items.summary: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, has exposed which nations are most exposed to disrupted energy imports.

items.title: Denmark faces data center reckoning as power grid overwhelmed by surging demand
items.summary: The data center industry is facing a reckoning as governments around the world consider imposing limits on the growth of the power-hungry facilities.

items.title: When survival is at stake, Bollywood production houses turn to distribution
items.summary: Bollywood production houses are increasingly entering the film distribution space over the past two years as a structural shift driven by rising production costs and changing revenue models.

items.title: India resumes wheat exports after four years but high prices likely to limit demand
items.summary: India has resumed wheat exports for the first time in four years, with ITC loading 22,000 metric tons for the UAE. A record harvest and higher global prices and freight rates are making Indian wheat competitive. This follows a ban imposed due to crop damage from extreme heat in previous years.

items.title: Samsung family pays off record $8bn inheritance tax bill
items.summary: The bill is tied to the estate left by the firm's late chairman Lee Kun-hee, who died in October 2020.

items.title: New Mexico seeks child safety restrictions on Meta apps and algorithms in trials 2nd phase
items.summary: New Mexico seeks child safety restrictions on Meta apps and algorithms in trial's 2nd phase

items.title: India is blocking a cheap way to beat the heat wave, one tweak in AC can lower costs
items.summary: India faces a cooling crisis as soaring temperatures drive unprecedented electricity demand. While AC sales surge, a safer, cheaper, and more efficient refrigerant, propane (R290), is being held back by outdated regulations. This overlooked solution could significantly ease grid pressure and reduce climate impact, yet its adoption is inexplicably slow.

items.title: UAE fertiliser giant resorts to trucks to shift product out of Gulf
items.summary: Abu Dhabi’s Fertiglobe switches to land cargo to avoid Strait of Hormuz as soaring prices allow it to shoulder extra costs

items.title: Gilt traders warn of ‘swing to the left’ as Labour faces electoral losses
items.summary: Investors warn loosening fiscal rules or change of prime minister could add to pressure on UK borrowing costs

items.title: America’s retail army now rules the stock market
items.summary: Unbowed by the Iran war and other shocks, individual investors are buying their way to new heights of influence

items.title: The land of milk and no money: UK farmers are in a fix
items.summary: Prices are falling due to a glut, yet the costs of feed, fertilisers and fuel are rising

items.title: Saudi Arabia touts new use for Neom port after Iran war
items.summary: With the Strait of Hormuz choked off, Riyadh focuses on Red Sea infrastructure

items.title: Wealth managers insist AI can work in their favour
items.summary: Fears about the technology’s impact hit share prices but sector is embracing the benefits

items.title: GameStop makes staggering $55.5 billion play to acquire online auction giant eBay
items.summary: GameStop has proposed a $55.5 billion acquisition of eBay, valuing it at $125.00 per share. GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen says he plans to lead the combined entity

items.title: Oil prices subdued after Trump says US plans to help stranded ships cross Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices held near $108 as Trump announced ‘Project Freedom’ to guide stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz, though blockade risks and fragile peace prospects kept markets on edge.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will 'free' ships trapped in Persian Gulf by Strait of Hormuz closure
items.summary: Vessels have been largely unable to pass through the Strait of Hormuz for months, disrupting key supply chains.

items.title: Rural job scheme demand dips by over a third in April—here’s why
items.summary: The decline in rural work demand in April may be a continuation of similar declines seen over the past few months, though it could also reflect implementation challenges with the VB G-RAM-G.

items.title: A key moment at Tata Trusts as it votes on the reappointment of two vice chairmen at its wealthiest affiliate
items.summary: An important fallout of the vote will be whether both Srinivasan and Singh can continue as vice-chairmen of Tata Trusts; an adverse outcome would prevent them from holding the titles, as the chairman and vice-chairmen must be trustees of all Tata Trusts.

items.title: Indian equities deserve higher FII allocation, but valuations stretched
items.summary: India merits a higher allocation given its structural strengths. While the underlying business is strong, the valuations are not attractive, said Ramneek Kundra, chief investment officer at DSP Pension Fund Managers. But India’s premium to emerging markets has come down.

items.title: EVs moving at faster speed in premium lane
items.summary: India's electric car market is seeing growth in premium segments. Carmakers are launching more expensive models, with sales in the ₹20-30 lakh bracket rising significantly. The share of cars below ₹10 lakh has decreased. Luxury EV demand is also strong. This trend suggests the market is expanding upwards, potentially limiting mass adoption.

items.title: White Whale bets on Blue Tokai, Atomberg via secondaries fund
items.summary: The Mumbai-based venture capital firm, which also backs startups such as M2P Fintech and iD Fresh, has completed the first close of the fund and expects to reach the final close within the next 12-18 months, co-founder Shapath Parikh told Mint.

items.title: Oil slips after Trump says US will help free ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices fell on Monday after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships in the Strait of Hormuz, yet remained above $100 due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10 amid stalled peace negotiations.

items.title: How Infosys is adapting as GCCs move outsourcing work in-house
items.summary: The Bengaluru-based IT major has mandated dedicated GCC offerings with embedded AI capabilities across its delivery centres, while also setting aside capacity within its campuses to incubate client-owned tech hubs.

items.title: Amsterdam bans public adverts for meat and fossil fuels
items.summary: Local politicians say the move is in line with the Dutch capital's environmental targets.

items.title: The threat to summer holidays looming from jet fuel shortages
items.summary: What impact might shortages have on our summer holidays - and what could be done about it?

items.title: In an Age of Doom Scrolling, Asia’s Book Market Is Thriving
items.summary: The internet was supposed to kill off books. But across much of Asia, a booming market tells a different story.

items.title: Tax shadow over charitable trusts with 'profit' motive
items.summary: Three top-notch hospitals in Mumbai and a famous international spiritual organisation-all functioning as charitable trusts-have been denied Income tax (I-T) exemption on the grounds that they were carrying out commercial activities.

items.title: MSME apprenticeships set for boost as India drafts roadmap
items.summary: India is embarking on an innovative strategy to enhance apprenticeship programs tailored for micro, small, and medium enterprises. Focused reforms in policies, regulations, and financial frameworks are on the table, with a group training model being a key contender. The goal is to equip the nation's youth with essential workforce skills by 2047.

items.title: Govt says auto fuel shortage rumour led to panic buys, assures stocks adequate
items.summary: While cooking gas supplies remained strained due to the West Asia war, household demand had been being prioritized and no shortages have been reported, the petroleum ministry said.

items.title: OPEC members increase oil production quota by 188,000 bpd, no word on UAE's withdrawal amid war in West Asia
items.summary: Saudi Arabia, Russia, and five other OPEC members increased their oil production quota by 188,000 barrels per day, despite the UAE's recent withdrawal. Analysts suggest this move aims to project stability amid ongoing market disruptions from the Mideast conflict.

items.title: In five charts: How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
items.summary: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

items.title: Nestle India flags potential price hikes as West Asia war impacts costs
items.summary: Nestlé India chairman and managing director Manish Tiwary noted that his team is focused more on things that can be controlled, such as cost optimization, than worry about external headwinds/

items.title: Voters will judge Trump on the economy - how is it doing?
items.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

items.title: Airlines can cancel flights in advance over fuel shortages under new plans
items.summary: Ministers hope move would help avoid last-minute flight cancellations for passengers this summer over Middle East fuel supplies.

items.title: Vedanta is changing its policy that assured at least 30% profit gets distributed as dividend
items.summary: Vedanta is replacing its mandatory 30% profit-distribution rule with a discretionary, board-driven dividend policy as it prepares to split into five entities, eliminating the predictable yield that attracted income-focused investors.

items.title: Will the West Asia war accelerate the age of electricity? Explained in charts
items.summary: The 1973 oil crisis dramatically changed the way that economies used fossil fuels such as petroleum. The ongoing US-Iran war could also reshape energy usage—for good.

items.title: Global week ahead: Is 'Sell in May' just a myth?
items.summary: Should investors 'sell in May' or flip a coin?
